content.full_text:
City Hall                                                    15900 East Main Street
Telephone (818) 330-451[illegible]                                    La Puente, California 91744


                                                                       [illegible]
                                                                       APR 0 '1991


                              March 27, 1991

PLEASE REPLY TO:
OFFICE OF THE CITY ATTORNEY
CITY OF LA PUENTE
3610 Long Beach Boulevard, 2nd Floor
Long Beach, California 90807
(231) 595-0072


Fair Political Practices Commission
428 "J" Street
P.O. Box 807
Sacramento, California 95804-0807

Attention: Blanca Breeze

Dear Ms. Breeze:

     As attorney for the City of La Puente and counsel for the La Puente
Redevelopment Agency, I respectfully request written confirmation
of your opinion rendered to John C. McClendon, Agency Special
Counsel Associate, on March 26, 1991 regarding the matter
hereinafter set forth.

     Manual Garcia is a member of the City Council of La Puente as well
as the La Puente Redevelopment Agency. Councilman Garcia's wife is
a school board member in a school district located in part within
the city of La Puente.

     The question, as posed to you by Mr. McClendon, is whether
Councilman Garcia must by law refrain from participating in any
Agency or City Council decision relating to a tax increment pass
through agreement between the Agency and the School District in
view of the fact that she is a member of the aforesaid School
Board. Your opinion, as orally rendered to Mr. McClendon, was that
in view of the fact that Councilman Garcia's wife is a school board
member does not disqualify him from participating in the previously
described Agency or City Council decisions. You further explained
that the key to your conclusion was Government Code Section


Fair Political Practices Commission
March 27, 1991
Page 2

82030(b)(2) which states that "'income' for purposes of the
Political Reform Act does not include 'salary reimbursement'
expenses or per diem received from a state, local or federal
government agency........" You further cited Section 82041 which in
turn defines "local government agency" to include a school
district. You opined that monies received by Councilman Garcia's
wife for service on the board of the school district, since it is
not deemed income, for purposes of the Political Reform Act, would
not be deemed a financial interest under Section 87101(c) of which
Councilman Garcia would have to excuse himself from the decision-
making process. Therefore, you concluded that the Political Reform
Act does not prevent Councilman Garcia from participating in Agency
or City decisions relating to the Hacienda La Puente Unified School
District of which his wife is a board member.

Please advise me if you need any further information. Your
cooperation in this matter is, as always, greatly appreciated.

Sincerely,

SAMUEL SIEGEL, CITY ATTORNEY
CITY OF LA PUENTE
AGENCY COUNSEL, LA PUENTE
REDEVELOPMENT AGENCY

SS:bh

McClendon, Agency Special Counsel Associate, on March 26, 1991 regarding the matter hereinafter set forth. Manual Garcia is a member of the City Council of La Puente as well as the La Puente Redevelopment Agency. Councilman Garcia's wife is a school board member in a school district located in part within the city of La Puente. The question, as posed to you by Mr. McClendon, is whether Councilman Garcia must by law refrain from participating in any Agency or City Council decision relating to a tax increment pass through agreement between the Agency and the School District in view of the fact that she is a member of the aforesaid School Board. Your opinion, as orally rendered to Mr. McClendon, was that in view of the fact that Councilman Garcia's wife is a school board member does not disqualify him from participating in the previously described Agency or City Council decisions. You further explained that the key to your conclusion was Government Code Section Fair Political Practices Commission March 27, 1991 Page 2 82030(b)(2) which states that "'income' for purposes of the Political Reform Act does not include 'salary reimbursement' expenses or per diem received from a state, local or federal government agency........" You further cited Section 82041 which in turn defines "local government agency" to include a school district. You opined that monies received by Councilman Garcia's wife for service on the board of the school district, since it is not deemed income, for purposes of the Political Reform Act, would not be deemed a financial interest under Section 87101(c) of which Councilman Garcia would have to excuse himself from the decision- making process. Therefore, you concluded that the Political Reform Act does not prevent Councilman Garcia from participating in Agency or City decisions relating to the Hacienda La Puente Unified School District of which his wife is a board member. Please advise me if you need any further information. Your cooperation in this matter is, as always, greatly appreciated. Sincerely, SAMUEL SIEGEL, CITY ATTORNEY CITY OF LA PUENTE AGENCY COUNSEL, LA PUENTE REDEVELOPMENT AGENCY SS:bh
